    <h1 {...{
      "id": "listening-to-inbox-events"
    }}>{`Listening to Inbox Events`}</h1>
    <p>{`This tutorial explains how to listen to events from the Inbox in your application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Messaging extension provides a way to listen to events from both the Inbox container and individual content cards within it. By conforming to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../public-classes/inboxeventlistening.md"
      }}>{`InboxEventListening`}</a>{` protocol, you can respond to inbox-level state changes and individual content card interactions.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "inbox-level-events"
    }}>{`Inbox-Level Events`}</h2>
    <p>{`These events track the overall state of the inbox container.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "onloading"
    }}>{`onLoading`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the inbox begins loading content. Triggered when the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` is first created or when a refresh (pull-to-refresh or programmatic) begins.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onLoading(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
    print("Inbox is loading...")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "onsuccess"
    }}>{`onSuccess`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the inbox successfully loads content. Triggered whether the inbox contains cards or is empty.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-1"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onSuccess(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
    print("Inbox loaded successfully")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "onerror"
    }}>{`onError`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the inbox encounters an error while loading. See `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../public-classes/inboxerror.md"
      }}>{`InboxError`}</a>{` for the error types that can be returned.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-2"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onError(_ inbox: InboxUI, _ error: Error) {
    print("Inbox error: \\(error.localizedDescription)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "content-card-events"
    }}>{`Content Card Events`}</h2>
    <p>{`These events track interactions with individual content cards within the inbox.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncardcreated"
    }}>{`onCardCreated`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a content card is created and configured. This is called once per card when the inbox loads or refreshes.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-3"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardCreated(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
    print("Card created: \\(card.id)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncarddisplayed"
    }}>{`onCardDisplayed`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a content card appears on screen. Use this to track card impressions.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-4"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardDisplayed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
    print("Card displayed: \\(card.id)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`This event is triggered automatically by the SDK when the card appears on screen. You do not need to manually call any display methods.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncardinteracted"
    }}>{`onCardInteracted`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when the user interacts with a content card (e.g., taps a button or link). The return value controls whether the SDK handles the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`actionURL`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`onCardInteracted`}</inlineCode>{` method provides an optional `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`actionURL`}</inlineCode>{` parameter associated with the interaction event. The return value of this method determines how the URL is handled.`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Returns `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` if your application has successfully handled the URL. This indicates to the SDK that no further action is needed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Returns `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`false`}</inlineCode>{` to allow the SDK to process the URL.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-5"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
    guard let url = actionURL else { return false }

    if url.scheme == "myapp" {
        navigateToDeepLink(url)
        return true // URL handled by app
    }

    return false // Let SDK handle URL
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`When a card is interacted with, it is automatically marked as read and persisted. The read status persists between app launches and affects unread indicator visibility (if enabled).`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "oncarddismissed"
    }}>{`onCardDismissed`}</h3>
    <p>{`Called when a user dismisses a content card. The inbox automatically removes the dismissed card from the UI.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-6"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardDismissed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
    print("Card dismissed: \\(card.id)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "implement-inboxeventlistening"
    }}>{`Implement InboxEventListening`}</h2>
    <p>{`Complete the following steps to listen to inbox events:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Conform to the `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../public-classes/inboxeventlistening.md"
        }}>{`InboxEventListening`}</a>{` protocol in your class or struct and implement the desired methods.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Pass the listener to the `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../api-usage.md#getinboxui"
        }}>{`getInboxUI`}</a>{` API.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p>{`Below is an example implementation of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventListening`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-7"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import SwiftUI
import AEPMessaging

struct InboxPage: View, InboxEventListening {

    let inboxUI: InboxUI
    @State private var showError = false
    @State private var errorMessage = ""

    init() {
        let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
        inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface, listener: self)
        inboxUI.isPullToRefreshEnabled = true
    }

    var body: some View {
        inboxUI.view
            .onAppear {
                Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([Surface(path: "inbox")])
            }
    }

    // MARK: - Inbox State Events

    func onLoading(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
        print("Loading inbox...")
    }

    func onSuccess(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
        print("Inbox loaded successfully")
    }

    func onError(_ inbox: InboxUI, _ error: Error) {
        print("Inbox error: \\(error)")
        errorMessage = error.localizedDescription
        showError = true
    }

    // MARK: - Content Card Events

    func onCardCreated(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
        print("Card created: \\(card.id)")
    }

    func onCardDisplayed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
        print("Card displayed: \\(card.id)")
    }

    func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
        if let url = actionURL, url.scheme == "myapp" {
            handleDeepLink(url)
            return true
        }

        return false
    }

    func onCardDismissed(_ card: ContentCardUI) {
        print("Card dismissed: \\(card.id)")
    }

    private func handleDeepLink(_ url: URL) {
        // Your deep link handling logic
        print("Handling deep link: \\(url)")
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "handling-actionable-urls"
    }}>{`Handling Actionable URLs`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`onCardInteracted`}</inlineCode>{` method provides control over how URLs are handled when users interact with content cards.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "handle-all-urls"
    }}>{`Handle All URLs`}</h3>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-8"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
    guard let url = actionURL else { return false }

    // Handle all URLs in your app
    navigateToURL(url)
    return true // Prevent SDK from opening URL
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "selective-handling"
    }}>{`Selective Handling`}</h3>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Swift" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "swift-9"
    }}>{`Swift`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`func onCardInteracted(_ card: ContentCardUI, _ interactionId: String, actionURL: URL?) -> Bool {
    guard let url = actionURL else { return false }

    // Only handle specific URL schemes
    if url.scheme == "myapp" || url.scheme == "http" || url.scheme == "https" {
        handleURL(url)
        return true
    }

    // Let SDK handle other schemes
    return false
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Avoid Heavy Work in Event Handlers`}</strong>{`: Event handlers are called on the main thread. Keep them lightweight and dispatch heavy work to background queues.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Handle Errors Gracefully`}</strong>{`: Provide user-friendly error messages and retry options.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Log for Debugging`}</strong>{`: Use event handlers to log state transitions during development.`}</p>
      </li>
    </ol>
